mnemonics: Deep Learning Optimizes Differently (DLOD): Think of DL as a chef who adjusts a recipe over many tastings (iterations), while a pure optimizer is a machine following a strict recipe to the letter.
keyPoints: DL training is empirical and uses data batches, while pure optimization often works with exact gradients of the entire function.
DL objectives are non-convex and noisy, making convergence to a global minimum unlikely.
DL prioritizes generalization to new data over perfect performance on training data.
commonMistakes: Assuming a found minimum is global; it is likely just a good local minimum.
Using optimization metrics (like training loss) as the sole measure of success, ignoring test performance.
